authoritative_sources.title: 2022 NAICS Manual: Sector 56 and NAICS 5614 Business Support Services
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The U.S. Census Bureau publishes NAICS and defines Sector 56 and NAICS 5614 business support services, including routine administrative and document-support services performed for clients, making it authoritative for the named industry and client-service boundary.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Industry boundary, client-service scope, and records-processing lane validation
used_for: administrative support and business-services industry boundary
business support services and records-support terminology
distinction between routine support processing and client decision authority
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents the package from becoming generic office support, broad client-operations ownership, or an official records-governance authority.

authoritative_sources.title: Occupational Outlook Handbook: Information Clerks
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: BLS is a U.S. Department of Labor statistical agency and directly describes clerical records duties, including maintaining records, collecting and recording data, filing paper or electronic records, entering and retrieving data, organizing records, and scanning or uploading documents.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Record intake, filing, retrieval, clerical update, and administrative record maintenance
used_for: paper and electronic record filing workflow
clerical data collection, recording, and maintenance terminology
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Defines practical records-processing work without implying authority over underlying case, benefit, legal, HR, financial, or official-record decisions.

authoritative_sources.title: 43-4071.00 - File Clerks
authoritative_sources.publisher: O*NET OnLine, sponsored by the U.S. Department of Labor
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: O*NET is sponsored by the U.S. Department of Labor and provides task-level workflow detail for storing, retrieving, updating, classifying, indexing, and tracking paper and electronic records.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Record filing, classification, indexing, retrieval, tracking, and maintenance
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Requires the lane to treat records processing as governed filing, indexing, retrieval, and maintenance work rather than broad records-management authority.

authoritative_sources.title: 43-9021.00 - Data Entry Keyers
authoritative_sources.publisher: O*NET OnLine, sponsored by the U.S. Department of Labor
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: O*NET is sponsored by the U.S. Department of Labor and provides task-level detail for source-document verification, data accuracy checks, logs, and completed-document storage.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Source-record verification, data accuracy checks, indexing, log maintenance, and completed-record handling
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Requires source evidence, logs, and QA before record metadata updates, handoff, or completion.

authoritative_sources.title: A Management Guide: Agency Recordkeeping Requirements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NARA is the U.S. federal records-management authority, and this guide explains record status, official file copies, file classification, related-record attachment, and electronic recordkeeping-system placement.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Record declaration, official-file placement, classification, and related-record attachment
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains the lane to verify record status, source context, classification, and official-file placement instead of inventing an unofficial repository path.

authoritative_sources.title: Universal Electronic Records Management Requirements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NARA is the U.S. federal records-management authority, and these requirements define record metadata, file plans, retention schedules, disposition, access, retrieval, integrity, and audit trail expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Metadata, retention, access, retrieval, audit, and disposition control
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains the lane to governed record maintenance and escalates retention, access, legal hold, and disposition decisions.

authoritative_sources.title: ISO 15489-1:2016 Information and documentation - Records management
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ISO 15489 is a recognized international standard for records management concepts and principles, including record creation, capture, metadata, maintenance, and use.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Record capture, metadata maintenance, and records-governance terminology
used_for: distinction between records processing and official records governance
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Supports the boundary between records-processing maintenance and official record governance or approval.

authoritative_sources.title: SP 800-53 Rev. 5, Security and Privacy Controls for Information Systems and Organizations
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST SP 800-53 is a recognized public control catalog for access control, audit and accountability, media protection, PII processing, and system integrity controls relevant to systems that hold client records.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Access, audit, privacy, media, and system-control verification
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Requires access, repository permission, audit-log, and privacy-sensitive handling evidence before distribution, update, publication, or handoff.

authoritative_sources.title: Disposal of Consumer Report Information and Records
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC's rule page summarizes 16 CFR Part 682, which governs disposal measures for businesses possessing consumer reports or related records for a business purpose.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Sensitive record disposal and records-governance escalation
used_for: routing to records, privacy, or compliance owners
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents the records-processing lane from approving disposal or destruction when consumer-report or sensitive-record handling authority is absent.

workflow_stages.name: Record intake and authority validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate client SOW, service catalog, requester authority, jurisdiction, business unit, data category, source hierarchy, record series, file-plan scope, and requested record outcome before work begins.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Source-record capture and evidence package assembly
workflow_stages.description: Collect source records, source documents, record images, OCR extracts, forms, attachments, control numbers, register entries, receipt channels, and batch manifests from authoritative systems.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Classification, indexing, and permitted maintenance
workflow_stages.description: Perform only delegated record metadata, classification, indexing, register, status, attachment checklist, version, official-file location, and exception-code maintenance tied to source evidence.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Retention, privacy, access, and governance control review
workflow_stages.description: Check retention class, record series, disposition instruction, legal hold, redaction, repository permission, privacy, access, and audit controls before handoff, distribution, publication, or disposal.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Exception handling, QA, handoff, and traceability closure
workflow_stages.description: Hold, rework, route, or hand off packages when required evidence is incomplete or authority shifts; produce QA and audit-trail evidence when package status is ready.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: Client SOW, service catalog entry, SLA, SOP, authorization matrix, and source hierarchy
Case ID, work item, batch manifest, source record, source document, record image, OCR extract, indexed fields, register entry, barcode, control number, and receipt timestamp
Required attachment checklist, signature, consent or authorization artifact, version label, redaction marker, classification, record series, file plan, official-file location, and retention category
CRM, ERP, HRIS, ECM, document management, records management, secure file-transfer, mailroom, scanning, OCR, workflow, IAM, QA, and audit-log records
Retention schedule, disposition instruction, legal-hold flag, access entitlement, repository permission, publication rule, and approved handoff rule
QA checklist, maker-checker review, exception code, rework reason, missing-item owner and due date, record update note, and audit-trail summary
systems_of_record: Document management or enterprise content management system
Scanning, OCR, indexing, mailroom, or secure file-transfer platform
Records management and retention schedule repository
CRM, ERP, HRIS, policy administration, customer master, vendor master, or client master system
Identity and access management and repository permission systems
Quality management, maker-checker, or audit review system
Audit log, security monitoring, legal-hold, and exception tracking systems

allowed: Determine whether available source records, source documents, required attachments, metadata, classification, record series, retention, access, QA, and audit evidence support blocked, incomplete, ready-for-indexing, ready-for-QA, ready-for-handoff, exceptioned, or routed status.

Update only delegated record metadata, classification, index, register entry, attachment checklist, status, official-file location, exception code, and audit-note fields inside tenant systems of record.
Produce records completeness summaries, administrative status notes, retention/access/governance escalation packages, evidence packets, and audit-trail summaries tied to named records.
escalate: Source records, source documents, OCR, records repository, DMS, workflow queue, client master, retention schedule, or tenant policy conflict materially.
Retention, legal-hold, access, privacy, redaction, publication, distribution, or disposal rules are missing or conflict with the request.
The request requires legal, licensed, privacy-rights, records-disposition, HR, finance, security, client-contract, policy-override, official-record, or final approval authority.
refuse: The request asks the lane to bypass missing prerequisites, source-record conflicts, approvals, access controls, QA failures, retention rules, or legal holds.
The request asks to decide the underlying case, claim, benefit, legal, HR, financial, or client-contract outcome.
Required tenant facts, authoritative source context, systems of record, record locations, or retrieval hooks are missing and would change the outcome.

domain_failure_modes: Accepting a record package without verified client SOW, requester authority, source hierarchy, or tenant service model.
Treating email, chat, local spreadsheets, or verbal assurances as substitutes for authoritative DMS, workflow, records, IAM, QA, or audit systems.
Updating official record fields without source evidence, delegated authority, approval record, or audit trail.
Choosing one source when the record image, OCR extract, case system, client master, records repository, or retention schedule conflict materially.
Distributing, publishing, disposing, or closing a package while retention, legal-hold, privacy, access, redaction, or publication rules are unresolved.
Collapsing administrative support records processing into generic office management or adjacent legal, privacy, records, HR, finance, security, IAM, or contract ownership.
Flattening tenant and client differences in SOW scope, jurisdiction, records platform, DMS platform, record series, retention class, QA rule, access rule, or data category.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Required record or evidence package is incomplete
Retention, privacy, or access rule conflicts with the request
Stakeholder asks the lane to approve an official record decision it cannot own

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Client-specific SOW, jurisdiction, data category, records platform, DMS platform, and retention differences
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Administrative support and business-services providers process records for many client programs, and tenant/client-specific SOWs, privacy terms, repository rules, retention schedules, access controls, file plans, and jurisdictions can materially change the workflow.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require runtime retrieval of client SOW, service catalog, jurisdiction, data category, systems map, record series, file plan, retention schedule, legal-hold status, access policy, source hierarchy, QA rule, and approval matrix before case-specific action.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Delegated authority for official record update, publication, redaction, distribution, and disposal
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Public baselines define evidence and control expectations, but tenants decide whether the lane may perform direct updates, only recommend changes, approve publication, or route disposal.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Retrieve tenant authority matrix and keep outputs recommendation-only, blocked, or escalated whenever update, publication, redaction, distribution, or disposal authority is unresolved.

notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for a validated spec pack because remaining ambiguity is explicitly constrained to tenant, client, jurisdiction, data-category, system, retention, access, QA, and authority retrieval at runtime.

The research output constrains the final pack to administrative-support records intake, source-record verification, file-plan placement, metadata maintenance, retention/access controls, evidence packets, and official-record boundary handling rather than generic filing or document assistance.
